skill.name: qwen-image
skill.description: Generate and edit images with Alibaba Qwen-Image-2.0 models through the inference.sh CLI, including text-to-image, multi-reference editing, custom sizes, negative prompts, and seed control.
skill.summary: Use inference.sh Qwen-Image models for image generation and image editing workflows.
skill.author: toolshell

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command, network, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns in SKILL.md. Review found no malicious intent or prompt injection, but confirmed that the skill instructs agents to run external CLI commands and send generation inputs to inference.sh, so it should publish with a medium risk warning.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Execution Required
medium_findings.description: The skill repeatedly instructs the agent to run infsh app commands and also documents npx skills add commands. This is a real external command risk because commands can start networked inference jobs or install related skills, although the primary allowed tool scope is limited to infsh and the commands are visible examples rather than hidden execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file clearly contains executable shell command examples for infsh and npx. The risk is bounded because the commands are documented workflow steps, not concealed or automatically executed code.

medium_findings.title: User Content Sent to External Inference Service
medium_findings.description: The skill is designed to call inference.sh hosted image apps, so prompts and reference image URLs may leave the local environment. This is expected for the advertised function, but users should avoid private prompts, confidential images, and sensitive reference URLs unless they trust the service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill explicitly names inference.sh and shows reference image URLs as input. I found no evidence of covert exfiltration, but external processing is inherent to the skill.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static weak cryptography findings occur in descriptive text, table labels, or prompt examples. No encryption, hashing, signature verification, or cryptographic API usage was found in SKILL.md at the reported locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are model descriptions, documentation table rows, or image prompt text. They do not contain cryptographic operations or weak algorithm selection.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Alert Is a False Positive
low_findings.description: The reported reconnaissance line documents the negative_prompt input option for image generation. No system probing, host enumeration, environment discovery, or privilege discovery behavior is present at that location.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is a parameter description for content to avoid in generated images. It has no semantic connection to local system reconnaissance.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Documentation and Example Inputs
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to the inference.sh website, an image preview, documentation pages, and example.com reference image placeholders. These URLs are not hidden endpoints and do not show credential or data exfiltration logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible Markdown links or sample reference image values. They are expected for a hosted image-generation CLI skill, though network use remains a publish-time warning.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bash Command Examples for Hosted Inference
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes shell examples that run infsh app commands with user-supplied prompts and image references. Agents should treat prompt content and referenced images as data sent to a third-party inference service.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command pattern is explicit and repeated. It is risky due to external execution and networked processing, but it matches the skill purpose.

dangerous_patterns.title: Skill Installation Commands in Documentation
dangerous_patterns.description: The related skills section shows npx skills add commands. These are installation commands and should require user approval before execution because they can fetch external packages.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The npx commands are plainly documented installation examples. They are not part of the declared allowed-tools scope, so they should be treated as manual user actions.

content.value_statement: Image teams need fast prompts, reference editing, and reliable text rendering without switching tools. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Qwen-Image workflows using inference.sh.
actual_capabilities: Runs Qwen-Image-2.0 and Qwen-Image-2.0-Pro through the infsh CLI.
Creates images from text prompts with optional size, count, seed, and negative prompt settings.
Uses one to three reference image URLs for multi-image editing workflows.
Shows when to choose the Pro model for posters and text-heavy image output.
Documents sample input generation and app discovery commands for inference.sh.
limitations: Requires an installed and authenticated inference.sh CLI before use.
Sends prompts and reference image URLs to an external hosted inference service.
Reference images must be accessible by URI for the documented editing workflow.
Output quality and latency depend on the selected Qwen model and service availability.

use_cases.title: Create campaign visuals
use_cases.description: Generate multiple image concepts with controlled size, style, and text placement for review.

use_cases.title: Prototype product imagery
use_cases.description: Use prompt variations and seeds to explore product scenes before commissioning final assets.

use_cases.title: Edit with visual references
use_cases.description: Combine subject, clothing, pose, or style references into a guided image editing request.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a simple image
prompt_templates.prompt: Use qwen-image to create a single image of a calm mountain lake at sunrise. Use a landscape format.

prompt_templates.title: Create several concepts
prompt_templates.prompt: Use qwen-image to generate four visual concepts for a modern coffee shop logo. Keep the style minimal and compare the results.

prompt_templates.title: Design a text poster
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced text rendering with Pro model
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Qwen-Image Pro to create a poster with the title "Spring Launch" and the subtitle "New tools for design teams". Keep the text exact.

prompt_templates.title: Edit from references
prompt_templates.prompt: Use qwen-image to edit three reference images. Keep the person from the first image, use the outfit from the second, and match the pose from the third.

output_examples.input: A request for four minimalist coffee shop logo concepts.
output_examples.output: The assistant selects the standard Qwen-Image model, prepares a prompt focused on simple logo shapes, requests four outputs, and returns the generated image links with brief notes.

output_examples.input: A request for a poster with exact sale text.
output_examples.output: The assistant selects Qwen-Image Pro, disables prompt rewriting for precise text control, and creates a tall poster layout with the requested title and subtitle.

output_examples.input: A request to combine a person, dress, and pose from three reference images.
output_examples.output: The assistant prepares a multi-reference edit and explains that the referenced image URLs will be sent to the external inference service.

best_practices: Confirm that prompts and reference images are safe to send to inference.sh before running commands.
Use Qwen-Image Pro when exact text rendering, posters, or detailed typography matter.
Set a seed when you need repeatable experiments or controlled prompt comparisons.
anti_patterns: Do not send private images, confidential brand files, or sensitive prompts without user approval.
Do not run related npx installation commands unless the user explicitly asks to install more skills.
Do not expect exact typography from the faster model when the Pro model is documented for that task.

faq.question: What does this skill use to generate images?
faq.answer: It uses the inference.sh infsh CLI to run Alibaba Qwen-Image-2.0 and Qwen-Image-2.0-Pro apps.

faq.question: Does it work without network access?
faq.answer: No. The documented workflow sends requests to hosted inference.sh image apps.

faq.question: Which model should I use for text in images?
faq.answer: Use Qwen-Image-2.0-Pro for posters, exact phrases, and text-heavy designs.

faq.question: Can it edit existing images?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documents multi-reference editing with one to three reference image URIs.

faq.question: Can it create repeatable outputs?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the seed option to make generation experiments more reproducible.

faq.question: Is it safe to use with sensitive data?
faq.answer: Use caution. Prompts and reference image URLs may be processed by an external service.
